rulings.Case Title: HAROLD GERECKE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION, LTD et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, OCTOBER 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s motion for judgment of dismissal is denied. C.C.P. § 581(f) is discretionary, and the court refuses to exercise that discretion in this instance. Plaintiffs provided a sufficient excuse for their delay in amending the complaint after defendant¿s demurrer was sustained with leave to amend. (See Contreras v. Blue Cross of Calif. (1988) 199 Cal.App.3d 945, 948.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT A. LINDSEY SR. VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, OCTOBER 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6. Defendant Union Carbide Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiff¿s interrogatory responses identified Mr. Lindsey, Jr. as a person with knowledge and stated what knowledge he had and defendant¿s moving papers failed to demonstrate that he does not possess such knowledge. Further, plaintiff¿s interrogatory responses cited to and summarized various deposition transcripts and defendant¿s moving papers failed to discuss them. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Lindsey, Jr.¿s declaration and the depositions of Mr. Hamilton create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to Hamilton Red Dot joint compound that contained asbestos attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JORGE DAVID VS. CHEVRON USA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Survival And Wrongful Death Actions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, OCTOBER 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Plaintiff¿s motion to consolidate survival and wrongful death actions is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff failed to provide proper notice by failing to add an additional two court days for electronic service as required by C.C.P. § 1010.6. Plaintiff may re-file the motion providing sufficient notice under C.C.P. §§ 1005(b) & 1010.6. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
